english: this is the biggest house in our neighborhood
mirad: His se ha gwa aga tam bi yata yubem.
notes: Hard: superlative, preposition bi vs be, noun form yubem

english: you must do it as quickly as possible
mirad: Et yefe xer gwa ig.
notes: Hard: degree expression gwa ig, verb xer (to do), imperative

english: this house is not as big as mine
mirad: Hia tam voy se ge aga vyel atas.
notes: Hard: comparative ge vyel, voy se, possessive atas

english: how glad I am that you came
mirad: Hoogla iva se at van et upa.
notes: Hard: exclamatory hoogla, emotion word iva, past upa

english: the whole world knows about you
mirad: Ha ayna mir te ayv et.
notes: Hard: ayv (about/concerning) vs be, ayna (all/every)

english: I think, therefore I am
mirad: At texe, av hus, at ese.
notes: Hard: ese (copula present), av hus (therefore), comma separation

english: what a day this has been
mirad: Hooa jub his saye.
notes: Hard: exclamatory hooa, saye (has been), his (this) vs hia

english: the baby is still sleeping
mirad: Ha tobud gaj tujeye.
notes: Hard: tobud (baby), still/sleeping morphology, progressive -eye

english: I swim better than you
mirad: At pipe ga fi vyel et.
notes: Hard: verb derivation pipe (swim), comparative ga fi vyel

english: I am not your father
mirad: At voy se eta ted.
notes: Hard: voy se (is not), possessive ted (father) not twed

english: it seems to me that
mirad: Tease bu at van.
notes: Hard: missing bu preposition after teaser, requires bu at

english: it does not bother me
mirad: Voy tepoboxe at.
notes: Hard: voy-prefix negation tepoboxe, dummy it not used

english: we would be going home but we would be happy here
mirad: Yat peyu tam, oy yat su iva him.
notes: Hard: conditional future peyu, oy (but) vs ay, su iva (would be happy)

english: we know that he or she will come and we will be happy
mirad: Yat te van it upo ay yat so iva.
notes: Hard: upo vs upu, su vs so (conditional vs future), it (gender-neutral)

english: we do not know where he or she went but we know where you live
mirad: Yat voy te hom it pa, oy yat te hom et tambese.
notes: Hard: complex embedded clause, oy vs ay, where-clauses

english: do they know where we went and where you live
mirad: Duven yit te hom yat pa ay hom et tambese?
notes: Medium: question particle duven, embedded where-clauses, hom placement

english: this teacher is good but that student is bad
mirad: Hia tuxut se fia, oy hua tixut se fua.
notes: Medium: hia/hua demonstratives, fia/fua adjective pair, clause conjunction

english: even if I disagree you will support me
mirad: Gey ven at yontexe, et bolo at.
notes: Hard: gey ven (even if), yontexe (disagree), bolo (support future)

english: you will never gain perfect happiness
mirad: Et hyoj ako fika ivan.
notes: Easy: future hyoj, ko/fi positive/negative quality, ivan (happiness)

english: my house is bigger than yours
mirad: Ata tam se ga aga vyel etas.
notes: Easy: possessive ata/etas, comparative ga vyel, regular morphology
